message:
fix(core): refresh sqlite_sequence on B2 fast path when AUTOINCREMENT tables exist

OPT-B2's schema-cookie fast path in reload_memdb_from_txn_with_mode skipped
the full sqlite_master scan when the schema cookie hadn't changed. But
AUTOINCREMENT INSERTs bump sqlite_sequence without bumping schema_cookie —
the cookie-match guard would therefore silently serve a stale
`sqlite_sequence_cache`, breaking `last_insert_rowid()` and the next-auto-
rowid hint for any strict-no-fallback connection with AUTOINCREMENT tables.

Match the sibling Lightweight-refresh path in
`try_refresh_prepared_metadata_if_stale` (line ~9668) which already handles
this: when `self.autoincrement_tables` is non-empty, re-read sqlite_sequence
from the caller-provided txn and overwrite the cache before returning.

Non-AUTOINCREMENT schemas pay zero extra cost (the borrow().is_empty()
check is cheap); the swarm_multiprocess workload uses INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
rather than AUTOINCREMENT so its bench numbers are unaffected.

Self-connection + autoincrement test suites all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
